assumptions: The analysis concerns consolidated financial statements of a group that is not an investment entity.
The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a recognised intragroup receivable/payable and is analysed as a monetary item under standard IFRS assumptions.
The identified exposure is foreign currency risk on the intragroup balance, not presentation-currency translation risk.

approaches.label: General exclusion of intragroup items in consolidation
approaches.reasoning: In consolidated financial statements, hedged items must generally relate to external parties; intragroup items are normally excluded. An intragroup dividend receivable is therefore not eligible unless a specific exception applies.
approaches.practical_implication: Absent a specific IFRS 9 exception, the exposure should not be designated in a consolidated hedge relationship.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.5 limits consolidated hedged items to exposures with external parties and generally excludes intragroup items.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to qualify in consolidated financial statements if the related FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable is a recognised intragroup monetary item
The counterparties have different functional currencies
The foreign currency gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
The foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss
The hedge relationship satisfies IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements
approaches.practical_implication: Assess whether the receivable's FX remeasurement survives consolidation; if it does, only that FX risk may be designated.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 allows foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item, such as a payable/receivable, to qualify when FX differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Paragraph 6.4.1 requires formal designation and documentation, an economic relationship, limited credit-risk dominance and an appropriate hedge ratio.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.reasoning: A separate exception exists for highly probable forecast intragroup transactions that affect consolidated profit or loss, but that model addresses forecast cash flows rather than a recognised dividend receivable.
conditions: The intragroup transaction is highly probable
It is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it
Its foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss
approaches.practical_implication: This exception is relevant only if the dividend flow is still a qualifying forecast transaction, not once a receivable has been recognised.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 separately permits certain highly probable forecast intragroup transactions to qualify in consolidated financial statements.
Paragraphs B6.3.5-B6.3.6 restrict this exception to cases where the foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss; many intragroup flows do not.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge in a foreign operation
approaches.reasoning: Net investment hedge accounting applies to foreign currency risk on the net assets of a foreign operation, not to a separate intragroup dividend receivable unless the designated hedged item is the qualifying net investment itself.
conditions: The hedged item is an amount of net assets of a foreign operation included in consolidated financial statements
The hedged risk is measured between the functional currency of the foreign operation and that of a parent entity
The same net investment risk is not designated more than once in the ultimate consolidated financial statements
approaches.practical_implication: A dividend receivable should not be analysed as a net investment hedge unless the designation is truly at the net-investment level.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraphs 10-12 limit net investment hedges to foreign currency risk on net assets of a foreign operation measured against a parent entity's functional currency.
Paragraphs 13-15 restrict duplicate designations in ultimate consolidated financial statements and require IFRS 9 documentation and effectiveness.
